rationale: The cited paper is directly about advertising deception, defensive processing, and distrust, which closely matches the citing paper’s focal constructs (deceptive/false claims and consumer demand response). The citing note explicitly positions its contribution against prior deception work as “largely laboratory based” and focused on beliefs/intentions, and the 2007 cited study is exactly a lab-experiment deception paper in that vein. This makes the citation plausibly foundational/theoretical rather than an obvious incidental mention.
